label: Total funding for air-quality projects 2015-2022
quote: A total of $330 million of known funding went to air-quality projects between 2015 and 2022. Since 2015, the level of investment to air-quality projects has more than quadrupled, indicating funders’ growing commitment. However, the level of funding from foundations to air quality in 2022, estimated at $71.3 million, has increased only slightly since 2021, suggesting a slowdown in year-on-year growth.
reason: Clear real-world funding amount and trend with specific figures.

label: Underfunding of air quality relative to all foundation funding
quote: Outdoor air-quality funding continues to account for less than 0.1% of all foundation funding; this comparative underfunding of air quality is a consistent trend between 2015 and 2022.
reason: Concrete percentage and trend claim about funding allocation.

fields.funder: Bloomberg Philanthropies, Clean Air Fund, C40 Cities

label: Breathe Cities funding at COP28
quote: At COP28, Bloomberg Philanthropies, the Clean Air Fund and C40 Cities announced a cohort of Breathe Cities to benefit from funding, technical support, air-quality data, community engagement, capacity building and additional assistance as part of a multi-year $30 million investment.
reason: Specific actors, event, and funding amount with real-world evidence.

label: Annual deaths from air pollution
quote: Air pollution causes 8.3 million deaths each year.
reason: Clear, impactful claim about human health impact.

label: Top funder categories for air quality
quote: Foundations focusing on climate, energy and environment (CEE) provide the highest amount of funding to air-quality initiatives, accounting for 77% of overall funding between 2015 and 2022. Foundations motivated by enhancing social equity, and those focused on health provide the second and third highest funding for tackling air pollution.
reason: Multiple named funder types with concrete share of funding.

label: Funding disparity in high-pollution regions
quote: Some of the regions with the highest air pollution receive the lowest funding, with Africa and Latin America received only 1% and 2% of the total funding (2015-2022).
reason: Specific regions with exact funding percentages, showing real-world inequity.

label: Funding through re-granters in 2021-2022
quote: In 2021 and 2022, more than $30 million of philanthropic foundation funding was invested through re-granters.
reason: Specific amount and time period for funding channel.

label: Recommendation to create new air-quality funding programs
quote: Creating a brand-new programme of air-quality funding, as exemplified by the ‘Clean Our Air’ challenge of the Earthshot Prize, or following the ambitious programme set out by another newcomer to the field, the Bezos Earth Fund.
reason: Concrete examples of real-world programs recommended as models.
